name: Anebulo Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
business: Anebulo Pharmaceuticals is a clinical-stage pharmaceutical company developing treatments for cannabis-induced toxicity, including acute cannabis-induced toxicity in children and acute cannabinoid intoxication in adults. Its lead product candidate, selonabant, is a small molecule antagonist of the cannabinoid receptor type-1 (CB1) intended to rapidly reverse the negative effects of cannabis-induced toxicity. The company has not yet generated any revenue and is prioritizing the development of an intravenous formulation of selonabant for pediatric patients.
sellsTo: Pediatric patients with unintentional cannabis poisoning

exposures.sentence: Our lead product candidate, selonabant (formerly ANEB-001), is intended to rapidly reverse the negative effects of cannabis-induced toxicity and reduce time to recovery.

catalysts.event: Initiation of single ascending dose (SAD) study of IV selonabant in healthy adults
catalysts.sentence: We initiated a SAD study of IV selonabant in healthy adults in the third quarter of calendar 2025.

catalysts.event: Pre-IND meeting with FDA to discuss development of IV selonabant
catalysts.sentence: We met with the FDA in December 2024 for a Pre-IND meeting to discuss the development of IV selonabant and the initial plan for clinical testing.

catalysts.event: Type B meeting with FDA to discuss Phase 2 data and Phase 3 path for oral selonabant
catalysts.sentence: We met with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (the FDA ) in July 2023 for a Type B meeting to discuss the Part A and B Phase 2 data and the potential path forward for Phase 3 development of oral selonabant for the treatment of adult ACI and received the minutes of the meeting in August 2023.

catalysts.event: Completion of Part C of the Netherlands Trial
catalysts.sentence: Dosing of an additional 20 subjects in an open-label extension of the study ( Part C ) was initiated in July 2023 and the study was completed in August 2023.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 1.8 million cannabis-related emergency department visits in 2021
tamClaims.sentence: Based on our evaluation of a published analysis of the most recent NEDS data, we believe that the number of cannabis related emergency department visits grew to approximately 1.8 million patients in 2021.
